slot: Small round cap on top-left corner of shifter console — pry off to access slot
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the small round cap on the top-left corner of the gear selector console.
Pry the cap off with your ignition key or a small flathead.
Insert the key into the exposed slot and press down firmly.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Move the shifter from Park to Neutral.

Battery is under the hood — connect a jump pack.
Once power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.

knownIssues.title: ZF 9HP Shift Lock Requires Power
knownIssues.detail: The ZF 9HP 9-speed transmission requires 12V power to shift. Without power the standard slot method is very difficult. Always carry a jump pack. A dedicated ZF 9HP override tool (~$50 online) is the most reliable mechanical option.

knownIssues.title: Shift Lock Slot Location Misidentified

knownIssues.title: Air Suspension Clearance Risk
knownIssues.detail: Air suspension can lower unexpectedly when power is lost or the vehicle sits for an extended period. Verify ride height is stable before attempting to load on flatbed. Some trims have a 'transport mode' — check if accessible.

knownIssues.title: Battery in Spare Tire Well
knownIssues.detail: 12V battery is located in the spare tire well under the cargo floor — not under the hood. Open the rear hatch and pull up the cargo floor to access jump terminals.

tc.type: Electric push-button (Autotrac) — 2000-2002 may have manual lever
positions: 2WD (2H) — rear wheels only, for all road towing
4WD Auto — automatic AWD engagement (NOT safe for towing)
4WD Hi (4H) — part-time 4WD high range (NOT safe for dry-road towing)
4WD Lo (4L) — low range — NEVER tow in this position
steps: Slow to under 3 MPH or come to a complete stop.
ELECTRIC (push-button): Press the 2WD button on the dash or console. Wait for the 2WD indicator lamp to illuminate.
MANUAL LEVER (older models): Stop completely. Move the transfer case floor lever firmly to the 2H position.
Confirm the 2WD indicator lamp is ON before lifting rear wheels.
Verify all 4WD indicator lights are OFF.
NEVER tow in 4H, 4WD Auto, or 4L — serious driveline damage will result.
IF BATTERY IS DEAD: Jump battery first to power electronic TC, shift to 2WD, then disconnect. If unable to shift, FLATBED ONLY.

towNotes: MANUAL TRANSFER CASE (floor lever): Vehicle must be STOPPED to shift. If lever is stiff, rock vehicle slightly in neutral while applying firm pressure.
ELECTRONIC TRANSFER CASE: Requires 12V power to shift. If battery is dead, jump the battery FIRST, then shift TC to 2WD before disconnecting.
DEAD BATTERY FALLBACK: If TC cannot be shifted to 2WD, USE FLATBED. Do NOT tow with TC in 4WD/4H/4L — will destroy transfer case and driveline.
AIR SUSPENSION: May lower/raise unexpectedly. Verify ride height is stable before loading on flatbed. Some models have a 'jack mode' or 'transport mode' — check with owner if possible.
HEAVY VEHICLE: Use properly rated wheel blocks, straps, and chains. Verify your equipment weight rating before attempting to load or lift.
4WD DOLLY FALLBACK: If transfer case CANNOT be shifted to 2WD (dead battery, mechanical failure), either use flatbed OR wheel-lift with dollies on the opposite axle.

knownIssues.title: Transfer Case Must Be in 2WD — Not Just Neutral
knownIssues.detail: Operators sometimes shift the transfer case to Neutral thinking that's safe to tow. The driveshaft is still connected in TC Neutral on these models. Transfer case MUST be in 2WD (2H) for wheel-lift towing, not just neutral.

knownIssues.title: Heavy Vehicle — Equipment Ratings

knownIssues.title: Battery in Frunk — Not Engine Bay
knownIssues.detail: The C7 Corvette has a front trunk (frunk) where the battery is located, NOT in the engine bay. Operators who don't know this will waste significant time looking for jump terminals. Open the hood and look in the front storage compartment.

knownIssues.title: Extremely Low Ground Clearance
knownIssues.detail: Standard ground clearance is approximately 4.5 inches. Lowered or Z51 variants are even lower. Any approach angle to a flatbed must be carefully managed with ramps or approach plates. Load from rear only — never winch from front over a standard ramp angle.

knownIssues.title: Carbon Ceramic Brakes — No Metal-to-Metal Contact
knownIssues.detail: Many C7 Z06 and Grand Sport trims have carbon ceramic brake rotors. These are extremely expensive ($15,000+). Never use metal wheel chocks directly against the rotors. Use rubber-padded chocks only.

battery.location: FRUNK (front trunk) — NOT in the engine bay. Open hood and look in front storage compartment.
battery.jumpTerminals: Inside the frunk — standard red (+) / black (−) terminals on battery
battery.warnings: Many operators waste time looking for the battery in the engine bay. It is NOT there. Open the hood — the frunk IS the storage area. Very low clearance — flatbed only.

slot: Cover near electronic selector on center console — remove to expose slot
shift: Press brake. Move electronic selector to N.
neutral: Battery: MID-ENGINE — access panel BEHIND passenger seat or in frunk.
notes: C8 is MID-ENGINE — battery location differs from all previous Corvettes.
warn: FLATBED ONLY for all C8. Extremely low clearance. Do not winch from front or rear.
towMethod: FLATBED ONLY — mid-engine, extremely low clearance. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle.
towNotes: LOW CLEARANCE VEHICLE: Use flatbed. Check ground clearance before attempting any wheel-lift — front splitter/rear diffuser damage is common.
DUAL-CLUTCH TRANSMISSION (DCT): Power is strongly preferred to shift to neutral. DCT without power may not release — use flatbed if shift override fails.

knownIssues.title: Mid-Engine — Battery Behind Passenger Seat
knownIssues.detail: Unlike all previous Corvettes, the C8 is mid-engine. The 12V battery is located behind the passenger seat or in the front storage area — NOT in the engine bay. There is no frunk battery on most C8 trims.

knownIssues.title: Absolutely No Winching From Front or Rear Bumper
knownIssues.detail: The C8 front and rear fascias have virtually zero structural tolerance for winching. Damage to the carbon fiber body panels is extremely costly. Use only designated tow hooks — front tow hook is accessed through the front fascia opening on the driver side.

knownIssues.title: Extremely Low Ground Clearance
knownIssues.detail: Ground clearance is approximately 4.5 inches stock. Z06 and ZR1 trims may be lower. Use approach ramps or extended ramps for flatbed loading. Approach angle is critical.

knownIssues.title: E-Ray Hybrid AWD
knownIssues.detail: C8 E-Ray (2024+) is a hybrid AWD. FLATBED ONLY for E-Ray. 12V terminals only — never access the HV front axle motor system.

battery.location: Behind passenger seat OR in front storage — depends on trim. NOT in engine bay (mid-engine vehicle).
battery.jumpTerminals: Behind passenger seat: pull seat forward panel to access. OR front storage compartment on some trims.
battery.warnings: Mid-engine — no conventional engine bay battery. E-Ray hybrid: 12V only, never touch HV front motor. Flatbed only.

knownIssues.title: ROTARY DIAL SELECTOR — Not a Traditional Shifter
knownIssues.detail: The 2015-2017 Chrysler 200 uses a rotary dial on the center console instead of a gear lever. Operators unfamiliar with this vehicle waste significant time looking for a shifter that does not exist. The shift lock release slot is at the BASE of the rotary dial.

knownIssues.title: ZF 9HP Transmission Issues
knownIssues.detail: This generation used the notoriously problematic ZF 9HP transmission. Neutral override can be difficult without power. A jump pack is strongly recommended before attempting any shift lock or neutral override procedure on this vehicle.

knownIssues.title: Door Handles Require Power
knownIssues.detail: The interior door handles are electronic and require 12V power to operate. With a dead battery, occupants can be temporarily unable to exit using the door handles. Be aware of this if the vehicle is occupied.

knownIssues.title: Extremely Low Ground Clearance — Side Lift Required
knownIssues.detail: The Viper has approximately 3.7 inches of ground clearance. Standard flatbed ramps cannot be used without damaging the front splitter and underbody. Side-lift equipment or very shallow approach ramps with extensions are required. Never winch from the front.

knownIssues.title: Most Vipers Are Manual Transmission
knownIssues.detail: The vast majority of Vipers have manual transmissions — no shift lock, no neutral override needed. Simply depress the clutch and select neutral. Automatic Viper variants are extremely rare. Verify before spending time on neutral override procedures.

knownIssues.title: 12V Battery in Frunk — Not Under Hood
knownIssues.detail: The F-150 Lightning has a front trunk (frunk) where the 12V auxiliary battery is located. The front hood opens but there is no conventional engine — the frunk IS the storage area. Pop the hood, and the battery is in the front storage compartment.

knownIssues.title: FLATBED ONLY — Electric Drivetrain
knownIssues.detail: The Lightning has dual electric motors driving all four wheels. Towing with any wheels on the ground will damage the motors. No exceptions — flatbed only.

knownIssues.title: Very Heavy Vehicle
knownIssues.detail: The F-150 Lightning weighs approximately 6,000-6,500 lbs depending on battery size. Verify your flatbed is rated for this weight. Many standard flatbeds are rated for 5,000 lbs and will be over capacity.

tc.type: Electric push-button on center console
positions: 2WD — rear wheels only — REQUIRED for road towing

steps: Stop or slow under 3 MPH.
Press the 2WD button on the center console.

steps: FOR WHEEL-LIFT TOWING: Stop completely. Shift transmission to Neutral.
Move TC floor lever to 2H. Confirm 2H indicator before lifting rear wheels.
FOR FLAT TOWING (all 4 on ground behind motorhome): Shift TC lever to N (Neutral).
Follow complete flat tow procedure in the owner's manual — ignition and transmission positions are critical.
NEVER tow in 4L on pavement.

FLAT TOW CAPABLE: Wrangler can be flat-towed (all 4 wheels on ground) behind a motorhome with TC in Neutral. Follow owner's manual procedure exactly.

knownIssues.detail: The JK Wrangler is one of the few vehicles that can be flat-towed (all four wheels on the ground) behind a motorhome when the transfer case is in neutral and the transmission is in neutral. However this is NOT the same as wheel-lift towing. For wheel-lift, TC must be in 2H. Confirm which type of tow is being performed before setting up.

knownIssues.detail: Rubicon models with Rock-Trac have a 4:1 low range ratio. If the TC is accidentally left in 4L during towing, the drivetrain will be severely damaged. Always visually confirm the TC indicator.
